text.title: Dataset Validation and Cost Estimation
text.summary: validate_dataset function: structure checks, token counting with tiktoken, cost estimation, and error reporting before upload.

rule.statement: Validate JSONL format locally before upload — check that each line is valid JSON, has "messages" key, and contains an "assistant" turn.
rule.rationale: The API rejects malformed files after upload; local validation prevents wasted time waiting for server-side rejection.

rule.statement: Count tokens with tiktoken before upload and display cost estimate to humans for approval.
rule.rationale: Training cost = total_tokens * n_epochs * cost_per_1M; a bad dataset can be expensive to discover after training starts.

example.note: Returns full stats dict including errors list. Caller checks errors before proceeding to upload.

list.item: Missing "messages" key — line is not an OpenAI chat format example
No assistant turn — training example has no target output for the model to learn
Invalid JSON on a line — JSONL requires exactly one object per line, no trailing commas
Content field is None or empty — silent token count of 0, model learns nothing from the turn

rule.statement: When generating training examples with GPT-4, manually review a random 10% sample before using them — do not auto-submit LLM-generated data.
rule.rationale: GPT-4-generated training data creates a feedback loop: the fine-tuned model inherits GPT-4's errors, blind spots, and phrasings as if they were ground truth.
